item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's actually two scips in series 4 slots that are in-deletion right now. Series 4 is on it's home stretch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow — this is officially the final SCP of Series 4.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I now see what you mean by the muddiness of the description, and I'll definitely fix that after the contest. The addendum is meant to convey "whaddaya know, the crazy plan actually worked; the Foundation got a skip in the bag by editing Avengers 2". It's less a one-time event and more "I think we caught most of em, good job". That addendum was a last minute addition so if that didn't come through I'd happily figure out a way to make that clearer.

I'm really not sure what more needs to be said about keeping the teeth themselves contained, to be honest; if you have suggestions for later I'm all ears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the first two sections of the description make it very unclear whether the anomaly is a phenomenon in the movie theater, or the theaters themselves. In addition, the final addenda feels like a letdown and further introduces confusion because it makes this anomaly seem like a one-time event and not a widespread anomaly.

The containment procedures feel lacking, you barely give any time to the most interesting part of the anomaly that would need the most unique containment procedures, instead focusing all but a sentence on the infiltration of the media which could be reduced down to a sentence itself. There's some really good imagery here but it's dragged down by unclear description and a focus on the least interesting parts of the concept. -1

item.title: JamCon Day 2
item.content:encoded:
Teeth have pulps, you see.

Thanks to rounderhouse, Mortos, Stallmantic, DarkStuff and MightyMcB for reading the draft.

Image is CC 3.0 sourced here: https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tooth_Roots.jpg
